availability.tooltip: Using your details, we will try to connect you with a person who is available at the same time as you. These are only approximate (for example, most weeks I am available on Wednesday evenings between 6pm and 8pm).

conditions.disclaimer: So far, our participants have had a positive experience - we want it to stay that way. If you have any questions please contact us at any time.
conditions.heading: Please read these principles carefully before you start!
conditions.info_text: When using Little World, there are some important principles to follow to protect both yourself and the people you are interacting with.
conditions.point1: Please be <highlight>polite in your interactions</highlight> with each other.
conditions.point2: This also includes <highlight>punctuality</highlight> and <highlight>reliability</highlight> for appointments.
conditions.point3: For your safety, please <highlight>do not exchange private contact details</highlight> (address, phone number).
conditions.point4: Any form of <highlight>discrimination</highlight> (including racism & ageism) <highlight>or harassment will not be tolerated</highlight>. Please report any violations to us immediately.
conditions.title: For a respectful interaction

donate.description: With Little World, we have created a digital platform where German learners and volunteer native speakers can easily connect, support one another and exchange ideas — all for free. Your donation helps keep this possible and enhances our services. This year, thanks to our sponsor, the German TV Lottery, every donation will be quintupled! But we need to hit our target to unlock this support. Every bit counts!
donate.subtitle: Help us connect 25,000 people to improve there German and create a more integrated society.
donate.title: Help us connect 25,000 people to improve there German and create a more integrated society.
error_view.title: Uh oh, this page doesn't exist
error.permissions: In order to join a call, you need to provide us access to at least one of your device's microphone or camera. Please check your permission settings.
error.reject_reason_min_length: Reason must be at least 20 characters
error.server_issue: Issue connecting to the server. Please try again.

q1.question: Can I choose the people I want to talk to?
q1.answer:
No, to protect the privacy of all users, nobody can see all members. But don't worry - you can still influence who you meet!

After you register, you will be required to fill out a short form. This allows you to indicate which topics interest you, your availability and any other preferences you might have. Our algorithm uses this information to then suggest suitable conversation partners to us and then we review these options and select the best for one for you.

q2.question: I don't have a match yet. Who can I contact?
q2.answer: First of all, sorry! We are working hard to get everyone matched as quickly as possible. Sometimes it can take a while. If it has been too long for you, please contact our support team and we will try to prioritse you immediately! In the meantime, please join our group meetings. They happen every Wednesday at 5.30 pm.

q3.question: I can't think of anything to talk about. What can I do?
q3.answer:
Then there's always the weather. Or penguins. Or pizza. And when you're done with that too, we've prepared some tools on the platform to help you during your conversations.

If you are in a video call with your partner on our platform, you will find the Questions button at the top of the chat area. Under the button you will find a series of questions that you can ask during the conversation. Try it out!

q4.question: Technical issues on Little World?
q4.answer: Whoops! If you have any technical problems, please contact our support and we'll take care of it right away!

q5.question: Camera, microphone or loudspeaker not working?
q5.answer: Please remember to grant access to the camera and microphone. Only with these permissions a video call can take place. At the start you will be asked for it, if this does not work, check your settings in the browser. You can find the respective instructions here: • Chrome • Firefox • Safari • Edge If it still doesn't work, write to our support and we will be happy to help.

q6.question: Is there anything I can do to prepare for the interviews?
q6.answer:
Before your conversation, just think about what interests you. Whether it's other cultures, everyday life, hobbies - the topics of conversation are as varied as you and your conversation partner.

Just try to find a quiet place with a stable internet connection to have your conversation. It's completely normal for the first conversation to not go perfectly. It can sometimes be a little bumpy at first - after all, you're just getting to know each other. Conversations with new people often take a little time to get into the flow.

And remember: learning means leaving your comfort zone. It may feel unfamiliar or challenging at first, but that's exactly what will take you further! Keep at it - it gets easier and more natural with every conversation.

q7.question: What if I don't like the match?
q7.answer: Sometimes the first conversation doesn't go perfectly - that's completely normal. That's why we recommend having at least three conversations before deciding whether it's a good fit or not. But that's just a recommendation. If you feel that it's just not right, let us know via the support chat. Sometimes it isn't and that's completely okay! We'll take care of it and find a new dialogue partner for you.

q8.question: My match isn't replying?
q8.answer: Sometimes it can take a few days before you receive a reply. If you do not receive an answer, please contact our support team. We can then try to contact your conversation partner for you.

q9.question: How do I start a video call on Little World?
q9.answer:
Starting a video call on Little World is very easy! Under the profile of the person you are talking to, you will find the orange ‘Call’ button with the camera symbol. Click on it to start a video call.

To ensure that the call actually takes place, it is best to make an appointment in the chat beforehand so that your conversation partner can prepare and accept the call.

Make sure that your browser has access to the microphone and camera so that you can see and hear each other. You can test this beforehand in the call preview to make sure that everything works.

q10.question: How long does it take to find a conversation partner?
q10.answer: For German learners, it can take about three weeks, as we have a lot of learners registering. As soon as we have found a suitable person, you will receive an e-mail with a suggestion. Please keep an eye on your e-mail inbox and spam folder during this time so that you don't miss your proposal! For volunteers, it is usually quicker, so expect a proposal from us within a week. If it takes longer, please get in touch with us - we'll take care of it! In the meantime, we recommend our introductory meetings every Wednesday at 5.30 pm. There you can become part of the community and get to know other users.

q11.question: First-name basis? Sie or du?
q11.answer: At Little World we are all on a first-name basis and when speaking German we use the informal "du" rather than "Sie". If the person you are talking to would prefer to be addressed using "Sie", it is of course polite to honour that. 😊

q1.question: Is it possible to have group discussions?
q1.answer: We'll be offering that soon! Theme-specific or completely free-style. We're looking forward to that! In addition, our community meetings take place weekly: To get to know each other and exchange ideas. Access and dates can be found in the section 'Home' and then 'Community Calls'.

q2.question: Does it make sense to exchange private information?
q2.answer:
It's entirely up to you! At Little World, everything remains anonymous and you don't have to share any personal data.

The platform offers a safe space in which you can communicate using your first names and on a first-name basis. Chat and video calls are integrated.

Real friendships often develop here, and many users then exchange their private data. But that's entirely your choice!

q3.question: Can I have several conversation partners?
q3.answer:
Volunteer native speakers can have as many conversation partners as they like. Every additional person you support helps us to ensure that German learners don't have to wait too long.

You can easily do this by clicking on ‘Start’ under ‘Conversation partners’ and then clicking on ‘Find a new partner’.

For German learners, we usually allow two active partners, as many are still waiting for a match and we would like to provide those without one first.

q4.question: Technical fault?
q4.answer: Suddenly nothing works anymore? If technical problems arise, please contact our support and we will take care of it immediately!

q5.question: Insult or harassment during a conversation?
q5.answer: This is absolutely not tolerated by us. Please contact our support immediately and we will act accordingly. You can also report a user and remove the match at any time.

q6.question: What if traumas break out or suicidal thoughts are expressed?
q6.answer: Please do not solve it yourself. In such situations, please contact our support and we will find professionals in the field to provide support.

q1.question: Can I also meet people in private?
q1.answer: If your counterpart is up for it too, you're more than welcome! After all, Little World wants to promote multicultural friendships that extend beyond our platform.

q2.question: Are there also Little World Community meetings?
q2.answer:
There are! We meet online every Wednesday at 5.30 pm for a relaxed chat, usually around a them, in order to get to know each other.

There is also a forum once a month where users and the Little World team meet. Here you can get the latest updates and propose suggestions for the further development of the platform directly with the team.

You can find access and dates in the ‘Start’ section and then under ‘Community Calls’. We look forward to seeing you there!

q3.question: Can I give feedback on the platform?
q3.answer: Feedback is always welcome! Feel free to write us a message in the support chat or use our contact form: https://home.little-world.com/kontakt.

q4.question: How can I delete my account?
q4.answer: Yes, you can. Log in to the platform and go to the settings page. There you will find the ‘Delete account’ button. Of course, we hope that you will stay with us. Do reach out to us with your feedback or concerns, and hopefully we can find a way to help you and improve your experience at Little World.

q5.question: How can I help Little World?
q5.answer: We are always looking for support. For programming, promotion, public relations and much more. We can use all the help we can get - donations help too. We have listed some of the best ways to support under Support Us.

q6.question: What happens at the end of the 10 weeks with my conversation partner?
q6.answer:
At the end of 10 weeks with your partner, it's your decision what you would like to do! We encourage you to continue speaking on Little World as long as you are both having fun.

Or you can start the search for someone new to speak to!

You're almost there. You can complete either option — finish your call or complete the walkthrough — to be eligible for a match.

partner1.info_text: These details are only visible to you and the Little World team.
partner1.language_level_tooltip:
The CEFR Levels:
A1: Understands and uses everyday expressions and simple sentences.
A2: Understands frequently used expressions on personal and everyday topics.
B1: Understands main points in clear standard language on familiar topics. 
B2: Understands complex texts and can express themselves spontaneously and fluently on various topics.
C1: Understands demanding texts and can express themselves spontaneously and fluently.
C2: Understands with ease everything read or heard and can reproduce information precisely and fluently.

partner1.tooltip: This relates to speaking ability only. A1 to C2 are common language certificates, although refugees from Ukraine, for example, are still at the beginning and usually have an A1/A2 level.

report.generic_description: We're sorry you’ve encountered an issue. Your report helps us review concerns and technical problems so we can take appropriate action and keep our platform safe and reliable. Thank you for your support

Your feedback helps us take appropriate actions to make our community even safer. Thank you!

resources.title: Resources
resources.beginners.title: Learning resources for beginners
resources.beginners.description: Um Deutsch zu lernen, empfehlen wir, an einem Deutschkurs teilzunehmen. Mit diesen digitalen Ressourcen kannst du den Kurs ergänzen.
resources.beginners.intro: Deutsch lernen ist einfach – mit diesen kostenlosen Websites und Apps, die wir für dich ausgesucht haben
resources.beginners.disclaimer: Sobald du das Niveau B1 erreicht hast, kannst du dein Sprachniveau bei Little World ändern. Dann können wir für dich einen Gesprächspartner suchen, mit dem du dich auf Deutsch austauschen kannst.
resources.beginners.apps_intro: Am besten ergänzt du die Kurse mit einer App. So macht das Üben Spaß und du bleibst dran. Diese Apps sind kostenlos:
resources.beginners.apps_1: Duolingo: Duolingo ist eine gute App, um spielerisch die Sprache zu lernen oder zu üben. Und man kann sie kostenlos nutzen.
resources.beginners.apps_2: Anton: Anton ist eine Schüler-App, wo alle Inhalte kostenlos sind und es keine Werbung gibt. Dort findest du „Deutsch Zweitsprache“ als eines der Fächer, die man in Stufe 0, 1 und 2 lernen kann.
resources.beginners.websites_intro: Diese Websites bieten Online-Kurse, um Deutsch in deinem eigenen Tempo zu lernen:
resources.beginners.websites_1: VHS Lernportal: Entdecke komplette Kurse für alle Sprachniveaus online oder als App, in 20 verschiedenen Sprachen verfügbar.
resources.beginners.websites_2: Learning German: Hier findest du eine umfangreiche Liste an kostenlosen Ressourcen und Online-Kursen.
resources.german.title: Learning resources for German
resources.german.description: Wir haben einige Ressourcen für das Deutschlernen auf den Niveaus A1-C1 zusammengestellt. Diese können das Lernen unterstützen, indem sie zusätzliche Arbeitsblätter, Aktivitäten oder Lektionen anbieten.
resources.german.podcasts_1: Easy German Podcast: Ein entspannter und lustiger Podcast, der das Deutschlernen leicht und unterhaltsam machen soll. Empfohlen für B1+.
resources.german.podcasts_2: Coffee Break German: Ein informeller Podcast im Stil einer Unterrichtsstunde, der verschiedene Grammatik- und Vokabelthemen behandeln soll. Es wird jedoch Englisch verwendet, um Konzepte zu erklären.
resources.german.podcasts_3: Slow German: Annik spricht langsam und leicht verständlich über aktuelle und interessante Themen.
resources.german.websites_1: DW Learn German: Kostenlose Online-Kurse, Ressourcen, Tests und Aktivitäten für Lernende von A1-C1.
resources.german.websites_2: Deutsch Perfekt: Kostenlose Online-Artikel und Tondateien nach Schwierigkeitsgrad geordnet. Ein großartiger Ort, um Deutsch zu lesen und zu hören und um Diskussionsthemen zu finden, die für verschiedene Sprachniveaus geeignet sind.
resources.german.websites_3: Deutsch Akademie: Kostenlose Online Ressourcen für alle Stufen. Voller Grammatik und Übungsaufgaben.
resources.german.websites_4: Lernox: Eine große Sammlung kostenloser Ressourcen, die Sie für bestimmte Niveaus und Themen durchsehen können. Zum Beispiel bestimmte Grammatik- oder Vokabeln Themen.
resources.trainings.title: Trainings
resources.trainings.description: Here you can find all of our training and workshops that help promote better communication and awareness.
resources.trainings.method: <bold>Lebenslanges Lernen:</bold> Lernen ist ein lebenslanger Weg. Unsere Trainingsmaterialien bleiben dauerhaft verfügbar, sodass du jederzeit darauf zurückgreifen kannst. Zukünftige Materialien werden hier ebenfalls veröffentlicht. Wir freuen uns darauf, gemeinsam zu lernen und zu wachsen.
resources.trainings.return: Return to the trainings overview page
resources.trainings.intercultural.descri: <bold>Stärke deine Kommunikationskompetenz:</bold> Dieses Training hilft dir, kulturelle Unterschiede zu verstehen und zu schätzen. Die kurzen 10-12-minütigen Impulse regen zur Reflexion und persönlichen Weiterentwicklung an. Du kannst die Themen einzeln oder nacheinander bearbeiten. Mit jeder Einheit stärkst du deine Fähigkeit, in allen Lebensbereichen flexibel, offen und positiv zu kommunizieren.
resources.trainings.intercultural.glossa: In diesem Glossar werden alle schwierigen Wörter aus den Videos einfach erklärt.
resources.trainings.intercultural.teache:
<bold>Über die Trainerin:</bold>
Raquel Barros, Leiterin der Werkstatt der Kulturen beim Diakonischen Werk im Aachen e.V., bringt viel Erfahrung in interkultureller Kommunikation und Zusammenarbeit mit. Lass dich von ihrer Expertise inspirieren!
resources.trainings.misunderstandings.de: Hast du dich schon mal gefragt, warum wir manchmal Menschen aus anderen Kulturen falsch verstehen oder vorschnell urteilen? In unserem lockeren Workshop gehen wir genau diesen Fragen auf den Grund, erklären viele Begriffe und geben praktische Tipps, wie man das Gelernte bei Little World anwenden kann.
resources.trainings.misunderstandings.gl: Hier ist ein Handout, das alle wichtigen Definitionen und Punkte zusammenfasst.
resources.trainings.misunderstandings.ha: Hier findest Du eine Zusammenfassung, wie Little World Dich dabei unterstützen kann, kulturelle Missverständnisse und Vorurteile zu überwinden
resources.trainings.misunderstandings.te:
<bold>Über die Trainerin:</bold>
Dr. Silvia Ruzzi ist Kultur- und Literaturwissenschaftlerin und hat an der Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin promoviert. Sie widmet sich insbesondere der interkulturellen Kommunikation und der Integration von Gemeinschaften.
resources.my_story.description: Every encounter is unique, and it is precisely these special stories that touch us and show us how beautiful diversity can be. This is something that should be shared! Let others share in your experience and tell us your story.
resources.my_story.disclaimer: <bold>Important note:</bold> You only agree to the publication of your story and pictures on our website, in our social media channels and newsletter after you have approved the story.
resources.my_story.examples: Curious to see what the stories look like? Take a look at some existing ones!
resources.my_story.intro: How long have you been chattting on Little World? What did you learn about yourselves? How did it feel? Were there any funny or particularly enriching moments? We are curious and would like to share these special experiences!
resources.my_story.outro_1: As soon as you have approved the story, it will be published on our website. We will also share it via our social media channels and in our newsletter to inspire as many people as possible.
resources.my_story.outro_2: You can also share the story yourself to help us encouraging others to join and contribute to a diverse and integrated society.
resources.my_story.step_1: Fill out the interview form together: Fill out the interview form together: Use one of your conversations to talk about your experiences. One of you can write down the answers while you chat. At the end, you can check together whether everything fits. Access the form here.
resources.my_story.step_2: <bold>Send us one picture of each of you:</bold> a portrait of each of you is perfect! It doesn't have to be professional - a good mobile phone photo will do. Just ask someone to take the picture for you (selfies are often not ideal). Make sure that the picture is not too small and that you are alone in it. Don't worry, we'll edit the pictures afterwards! You can upload your images using the file selector at the bottom of the page.
resources.my_story.step_3: <bold>We will create a short story and a nice image</bold> We will create a short story and a nice image from your photos and answers. You will receive both by e-mail for approval before we publish them.
resources.my_story.steps_intro: It's easy - it only takes three simple steps:
resources.my_story.submit_error: Issue uploading images. Please try again
resources.my_story.subtitle: How it works?
resources.my_story.title: Tell us your story!

resources.partners.babbel.description: A free intensive online German course for A1/A2 learners living in Germany or Austria, designed to help participants reach B1 level in 6–9 months. The course includes daily online classes (Monday–Friday) and self-study via the Babbel app. It is available in English or Ukrainian, and a full-time commitment is required. Starts on the 23rd of June 2025.
resources.partners.babbel.text_content:
Little World is helping Babbel find dedicated A1/A2 German learners for a fantastic opportunity: a free, full-time intensive language course designed to take you to B1 level in just 6–9 months.

This course requires you to attend weekday classes (2 hours each day) for the duration of the course. To give you more flexibility, you can choose between morning and evening classes.

<bold>🧑‍🏫 What’s included in the course?</bold>
- Daily live online classes (Mon–Fri, 2 hours/day) led by experienced teachers
- Self-study with the Babbel app to boost your grammar, vocab & pronunciation
- AI-powered speaking practice, anytime and anywhere
- Cultural insights & practical info for life and work in German-speaking countries
- Exam preparation for official B1 German exams (exam registration not included)

<bold>📅 Course format</bold>
- Start date: 23rd June 2025
- Duration: 6–9 months, depending on your starting level
- You’ll take a placement test to find the right level (A1.1 to A2.1)
- Each module lasts 6 weeks
- Two time slots to choose from:
  👉 9:30–11:30 CET
  👉 17:00–19:00 CET

<bold>💸 Cost</bold>
- Normally €1,380 for the full course
- Free for selected participants
- €200 deposit required, fully refundable if you complete the full course

<bold>✅ You can apply if you:</bold>
- Are living in Germany or Austria
- Are not enrolled in or eligible for a government integration course
- Have A1 or A2-level German
- Can commit to daily attendance and self-study for 6–9 months
- Speak basic English or Ukrainian
- Have a smartphone or computer with internet
- Are willing to participate in feedback (e.g. surveys, interviews)

👉 This is a unique opportunity to reach B1  – don’t miss out!
If you have any questions please reach out to us via the support chat.
resources.partners.lernfair.description: Lern-Fair e.V. has been offering free, digital learning support for educationally disadvantaged students since 2020. With private learning support for students with learning deficits and less access to education or offering group courses with a different focus, they aim to give students throughout Germany free and unbureaucratic access to digital educational opportunities and thus create educational opportunities. Their major goal is to give all students the same opportunities for education.
resources.partners.lernfair.text_content: Lern-Fair e.V. has been offering free, digital learning support for educationally disadvantaged students since 2020. With private learning support for students with learning deficits and less access to education or offering group courses with a different focus, they aim to give students throughout Germany free and unbureaucratic access to digital educational opportunities and thus create educational opportunities. Their major goal is to give all students the same opportunities for education.
resources.partners.lewagon.description: Looking to start a career in Data & AI? Want to join a field that is challenging, in-demand and highly paid? Through Le Wagon's fully funded Data & AI course, make it a reality.
resources.partners.lewagon.text_content: Your pathway into IT! We’re excited to present you with this amazing opportunity! In partnership with Le Wagon, we’re helping you step into the IT world with a hands-on Data & AI course, fully funded by an education voucher from the Employment Agency or Job Center (completely free for you). In this online course, you'll study intensively for 10 months and then gain 2 months of hands-on experience at an IT company – a full year that will fully prepare you for your new career. Who is this course for? 🌍 People with a migration background looking to change careers. 👩‍💻 Women in tech – we especially encourage women to take this step into IT. 🛂 Refugees with work permits, who want to start their careers in Germany. 🎓 Academics with foreign degrees seeking better opportunities. 🗣️ People with good English (approx. B2) and basic math skills. 🤝 Job seekers in Germany looking to transition to a future-proof industry. 💡 Not for you? Share this opportunity with others! Together, we’ll make sure more people hear about this chance. What makes this course special? • Hands-on & future-oriented – Learn to solve real-world problems with data. • Close connection to the job market – Le Wagon has a strong network with companies. • English as the course language – Plus: Optional German training. • Easy access – If you’re not yet registered with the Job Center or Employment Agency, Le Wagon can help you with the application for the education voucher. • Community & network – Become part of a global tech network. Why we recommend this course? Our mission is to promote inclusion & diversity. A career perspective is key to true integration. The IT industry is diverse, international, and future-proof – and it needs talents like you! 📢 Interested? 👉 Join our Info-Call with Le Wagon on April 10th at 5 PM CET! You’ll get all the details and can ask any questions you have. 📖 Want to know more? Here you can find all the info and register directly with Le Wagon:
searching_card.matched_info_1: We are busy finding the best match for you. You'll receive a notification once we have!
searching_card.matched_info_2: Please note this can take a few weeks, so we appreciate your patience.
searching_card.pre_match_call_booked_inf: Your introductory video call will take place via Zoom at:
searching_card.onboarding_completed_info: We are busy finding the best match for you. You'll receive a notification once we have!
Please note this can take a few weeks, so we appreciate your patience.
searching_card.pre_match_call_not_booked: Before we match you, we would like to get to know you in a short 30-minute video call as a group.
We'll answer your questions, give you a few tips, and help safeguard you and the rest of our community.
self_info.gender_tooltip: In order to create a pleasant and safe atmosphere for conversation, we also take cultural and personal wishes into account, e.g. the gender of the other person.
self_info.language_skills_label: What is your language level? Only related to speaking.
self_info.language_skills_tooltip: For better conversations, we try to connect you with a person who potentially speaks the same languages.
self_info.country_of_residence_tooltip: Currently Little World's priority is matching & supporting people that live or are about to live in Germany. If you are living outside of Germany, there's still good reason to sign up with us - our group sessions, workshops and resources are open to everyone.
self_info.country_of_residence_warning: Currently Little World's priority is matching & supporting people that live or are about to live in Germany. If you are living outside of Germany, there's still good reason to sign up with us - our group sessions, workshops and resources are open to everyone.
self_info.post_code_tooltip: As a non-profit organization, we provide regional supporters with transparent and anonymous information about our regional impact. For example, "54 people in Atlantis". In addition, we will soon also be providing information in person.
self_info.target_group_label: Select the group you most align with:
self_info.target_group_tooltip: e.g. students, pensioners, jobseekers, ... this information helps us to measure our impact and better understand the needs in our society.
self_info.title: About Yourself

sign_up.birth_year_tooltip: You have to be at least 18 years old to join Little World.
sign_up.company_tooltip: If you are using Little World on behalf of your company, please enter the name of your company.
sign_up.mailing_list_label: I would like to receive the Little World newsletter
sign_up.user_type_tooltip: Choose how you'd like to participate on the platform. Please note: to join as a volunteer conversation partner, you need at least a C1 level or must be a native speaker.
sign_up.privacy_policy: Please take note of our privacy policy
sign_up.title: Create account
support_us.advanced_ways.title: Advanced Ways to Help
support_us.become_a_volunteer.descriptio: Click on "Find a Conversation" to assist someone in improving their German skills. If you're a C1-level German learner, you can volunteer to help someone at a lower level by adjusting your search criteria after clicking on “Find a Conversation”. Your support can make a significant difference in their learning journey.
support_us.become_a_volunteer.title: Help Another Person as a Volunteer
support_us.corporate_involvement.descrip: Persuade your company to donate to Little World, participate in corporate volunteering, or provide other forms of support. Feel free to share our One-Pager and website. Contact us for additional information and any way we can support you.
support_us.corporate_involvement.title: Corporate Involvement
support_us.description: Our utopia is a society where unexpected friendships happen and where diversity is celebrated. We firmly believe this is achievable. We are leading the way – but as a small team with limited resources as a Non-Profit, we need you to walk this path with us and support us to achieve this vision. Here are all the ways to support us:
support_us.distribute.description: Distribute our materials in places like cafes, bars, doctors' offices, or hairdressers. These places are often willing to showcase our materials. Sign up with your address and name and we will send you posters, flyers, and/or postcards for free.
support_us.distribute.title: Distribute Promotional Materials
support_us.donate.description: Support Little World with a financial contribution or donate goods for welcome presents to immigrants. As a non-profit organization, your donations help us continue our work and support immigrants in Germany.
support_us.donate.title: Donate
support_us.get_interviewed.description: Share your story and experiences with Little World by getting interviewed for our blog or podcast. This helps us attract more participants. We will only mention your first name or use a fictional name if you prefer to remain anonymous.
support_us.get_interviewed.title: Get Interviewed
support_us.intermediate_ways.title: Intermediate Ways to Help
support_us.join_events.description: Participate in our group events to connect with our community, learn, and share your experiences.
support_us.join_events.title: Join Group Events
support_us.join_groups.description: Typically only 5-10% of your followers on social media see your post. To enhance our visibility, we have created WhatsApp and Signal groups where we share direct links to our posts for quick engagement. Fast engagement after posting significantly improves our reach. Join our WhatsApp and Signal groups to stay updated and spread some love to help boost our visibility.
support_us.join_groups.title: Join Our Engagement Groups
support_us.organize_an_event.description: Share your passion, knowledge, or the unique aspects of your home country with our community by hosting a group event. Events can range from 5 to 30 minutes and provide a great opportunity to connect, practice German, learn new things, and discover interesting conversation topics.
support_us.organize_an_event.title: Organize Your Own Group Event
support_us.share_improvements.descriptio: We value your input! Share your ideas on how we can make Little World better for everyone. Additionally, help us improve Little World by reporting any bugs or issues you encounter. Please mention the device and browser you are using in order for us to fix it accordingly.
support_us.share_improvements.title: Share Improvement Ideas
support_us.share_with_friends.descriptio: Help us spread the word about Little World via word of mouth, social media, WhatsApp, or email newsletters. Find a variety of text and image templates in our Media Kit.
support_us.share_with_friends.title: Share with Friends
support_us.simple_ways.title: Simple Ways to Help
support_us.social_media.description: Follow, like, and comment on our social media posts. Your engagement helps us reach more people. Don’t forget to turn on notifications for our posts and to select “See first” on Facebook.
support_us.social_media.title: Follow and Engage on Social Media
support_us.subscribe_to_newsletter.descr: Stay updated with the latest news and events from Little World by subscribing to our newsletter. You'll know our progress as a community and learn how you can further contribute.
support_us.subscribe_to_newsletter.title: Subscribe to Our Newsletter
support_us.thank_you.message: Thank you so much for your support ❤️ ❤️ ❤️
support_us.thank_your_partner.descriptio: Thank your match and express your appreciation for the conversations. Learners especially can thank their volunteers. Showing gratitude and appreciation is the best way to keep everyone motivated, engaged, and make someone's day a bit brighter.
support_us.thank_your_partner.title: Thank Your Conversation Partner
support_us.title: Reaching a better society together
support_us.volunteer.description: Become a valuable member of our enthusiastic and supportive team! We welcome everyone and can use all volunteers, regardless of your background or skill set. We are particularly seeking volunteers with skills in building cooperations ("sales"), programming, social media, or marketing. Your support is crucial in helping us achieve our vision of a utopian society. Join our daily team meetings on Zoom at 12:00 PM (Monday to Friday) or fill out our quick form to get in touch with us and start making a difference.
support_us.volunteer.title: Volunteer in Our Dynamic Team
support_us.write_a_review.description: Share your positive experiences with Little World by writing a five-star review on Google. For improvement suggestions, please use our support chat or the "Help" section form.
support_us.write_a_review.title: Write a Review on Google

unmatch.partner_description: Unmatching is permanent. If you think we might be able to help improve the situation, please reach out to Support first - we’re here to help you!
update_search_modal.idle.description: By clicking confirm you will be set to searching. This tells us that you are looking for a match, which we'll do our best to do.
update_search_modal.idle.title: Reactivate search
update_search_modal.searching.descriptio: By clicking confirm, we will stop searching for a new match for you and therefore won't receive any new match proposals from us. This will not affect any existing matches that you currently have
update_search_modal.searching.title: Cancel search
